what_it_does: Build.jsx is a React canvas that turns a user-written design brief into a reviewed AI plan, runs parallel specialist subtasks (icons, colors, copy, widgets, wizards, workshop docs, and type drafts), composes the results into a preview, and supports scoped refinement and adoption of generated assets.
observations: Provides seven seed briefs (brochure, palette, icons, voice, slide, widget, wizard).
Implements a four-stage pipeline: brief, plan review, generation, compose/adopt.
Planner prompt asks Vi to emit a JSON plan with exactly one composer subtask at the end.
Subtasks carry a status string: idle, active, done, or blocked.
Non-composer subtasks run in parallel via Promise.all; the composer runs after they finish.
Includes two refinement paths: refinePart for scoped per-item/section/composed edits, and refine for free-text triage that decides which subtasks to rerun.
Uses many window globals: CV_AI, viDraftWidget, viDraftWizard, WS_viDraft, CV_TYPES_VI, TypeLayerBadge, CV_TYPES_PROMPT, WidgetRender, dsaToast.
Renders AI-generated SVG icon bodies with dangerouslySetInnerHTML.
saveTemplate asks the AI to extract parameterized placeholders from the brief.
WorkshopDocResult can open generated widget/wizard/deck/brochure docs in Workshop.
Heavy use of inline style objects and dsa-* CSS class names.
standouts: Dual refinement UI: per-asset RefineDot chips and section-level RefineChip controls, plus a separate triage-based refine flow.
Plan editor lets users edit labels/briefs and remove subtasks before generation.
Type-builder integration proposes a new Type Registry entry and opens a registration prompt.
Composer assembles a generic preview card from icons, colors, and copy variants.
conventions: React functional components with useState/useEffect/useRef hooks.
Global window services for AI and domain engines.
AI prompts request compact JSON only, with regex-based fallback parsing.
Inline style objects keyed to CSS custom properties.
Component exported to window.* for runtime registration.
concerns: dangerouslySetInnerHTML injects AI-generated SVG markup without sanitization.
Relies on numerous window globals that are not defined or imported in this file.
Output JSX contains a stray '/>}' after the <Output/> call, which looks like a syntax error.
refine() reads plan from closure while setPlan updates are asynchronous, so affected subtask briefs/status may be stale during rerun.
refinePart uses a setPlan callback hack to read 'latest' plan state, which is fragile.
subtasksRef.current is reassigned on every render, defeating the purpose of a ref.
No validation that parsed AI results contain expected fields (icons, colors, variants).
No error boundaries or fallback UI for blocked subtasks beyond status styling.
notes: This is the Build canvas — an AI-orchestrated brief-to-assets pipeline with plan review, parallel generation, composition, and scoped refinement.
questions: What is the exact contract for window.CV_AI.execute versus window.CV_AI.complete?
How are onAdoptIcons and onAdoptColors wired into the rest of the app?
Are the global draft functions (viDraftWidget, viDraftWizard, WS_viDraft) guaranteed to be loaded before Build is mounted?
What should happen when a subtask is blocked — should the composer still run with partial data?
Is the stray '/>}' in the stage 4 JSX a typo or an intentional fragment marker?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is a cross-canvas Vi planner with a 3-layer task tree; the file actually implements a four-stage brief-to-output pipeline with plan editing, parallel specialist execution, composition, and refinement, which aligns closely with the stated intent.
apparent_themes: Composable design assets generated by specialist agents.
Scoped iterative refinement of generated parts.
Single-file canvas components with inline styling.
novelty: The combination of a planner-generated task tree, per-asset refinement dots/chips with scoped section edits, and a separate triage-based free-text refinement flow is distinctive.
contribution: A concrete, file-local implementation of a brief-to-assets orchestration canvas, including plan editing UI, parallel subtask execution, composed preview rendering, scoped refinement controls, and adoption/template hooks that a fused system can wire into a broader design-studio runtime.
summary_for_embedding: Build.jsx is a React canvas that turns a textual design brief into a reviewed, AI-decomposed plan of parallel specialist subtasks (icons, colors, copy, widgets, wizards, workshop docs, types), runs them, composes a preview, and supports scoped refinement and adoption.
intention_for_embedding: To give users a single 'build' surface where a planner agent decomposes requests, specialist agents generate parts, and a composer assembles adoptable design outputs.

symbols.does: Root React component for the Build canvas.
symbols.description: Build manages the four-stage pipeline state (brief, plan, generation, compose), renders the appropriate stage UI, and exposes handlers for adoption, saving templates, and opening workshop docs.

symbols.does: Calls the AI planner to decompose the brief into subtasks.
symbols.description: generatePlan sends a structured prompt to window.CV_AI.execute('build.plan'), parses the returned JSON, validates that subtasks exist, and stores them with idle status before advancing to stage 2.

symbols.does: Updates a subtask's brief string in plan state.
symbols.description: updateSubtaskBrief produces a new plan object with the matching subtask's brief replaced, used by the plan editor's textarea inputs.

symbols.does: Updates a subtask's human-readable label in plan state.
symbols.description: updateSubtaskLabel returns a new plan with the matching subtask's label replaced, used by the plan editor's input fields.

symbols.does: Removes a subtask from the plan.
symbols.description: removeSubtask filters the subtasks array so the plan editor can drop unwanted specialist tasks before generation.

symbols.does: Executes all non-composer subtasks in parallel.
symbols.description: runGeneration advances to stage 3, marks each non-composer subtask active, runs them in parallel via Promise.all, updates their status/results, and then automatically calls runCompose.

symbols.does: Runs the composer subtask using generated results.
symbols.description: runCompose advances to stage 4, marks the composer active, invokes runComposer with the current parts, and records the composed result or a blocked error.

symbols.does: Re-runs the composer without regenerating parts.
symbols.description: recompose gathers the current non-composer results and calls runCompose again, useful when only the final assembly needs to change.

symbols.does: Returns the canvas to the initial brief stage.
symbols.description: reset clears the plan, error, refinements, and input, and sets stage back to 1.

symbols.does: Navigates back to the brief stage if not busy.
symbols.description: backToBrief guards against running operations and returns the user to stage 1 to edit the brief.

symbols.does: Navigates back to the plan review stage if not busy.
symbols.description: backToPlan guards against running operations and returns the user to stage 2 to edit subtasks.

symbols.does: Performs a scoped refinement on a single asset, section, or composition.
symbols.description: refinePart handles icon, color, copy-variant, icons-all, colors-all, copy-all, and composed scopes by prompting the AI and updating the relevant subtask result, then re-runs the composer to reflect the change.

symbols.does: Sets a subtask's status to active in plan state.
symbols.description: markActive is a local helper closure used inside refinePart to visually flag a subtask while it is being regenerated.

symbols.does: Sets a subtask's status to done with a result in plan state.
symbols.description: markDone is a local helper closure used inside refinePart to store a new result and clear the active state for a subtask.

symbols.does: Free-text refinement that triages which subtasks to rerun.
symbols.description: refine asks the AI to decide which subtasks need changes, overrides their briefs, reruns them in parallel, and then recomposes the output while recording a revision entry.

symbols.does: Renders the four-stage progress bar.
symbols.description: Stepper displays Brief, Plan, Generate, and Compose steps, marks completed and active steps, and allows jumping back to earlier completed stages when not busy.

symbols.does: Renders the editable subtask grid.
symbols.description: PlanEditor lays out non-composer subtasks in a responsive grid and places the composer card below them with a connecting visual line.

symbols.does: Renders a single editable subtask card.
symbols.description: PlanEditorCard shows the subtask kind, an editable label input, an editable brief textarea, and a remove button for non-composer tasks.

symbols.does: Shows live previews of generated parts during stage 3.
symbols.description: PartsPreview renders icon thumbnails, color strips, copy variants, and type-draft cards as their respective subtasks complete.

symbols.does: Renders one of the four explanatory cards on the brief stage.
symbols.description: BuildHowCard is a small presentational component that explains a layer of the Build pipeline with a title and description.

symbols.does: Renders the parallel subtask status grid.
symbols.description: TaskList displays non-composer subtasks side by side and the composer below them, each rendered as a TaskNode with status styling.

symbols.does: Renders a single subtask status card.
symbols.description: TaskNode shows the subtask kind, label, brief, and a colored status indicator that pulses when active.

symbols.does: Renders the final composed output and adoptable assets.
symbols.description: Output displays the composed preview, workshop-doc results, generated icons/colors/copy with adopt buttons, per-asset refine controls, and a save-as-template action.

symbols.does: Saves the current run as a reusable template.
symbols.description: saveTemplate asks the AI to extract parameterized placeholders from the brief, then calls onSaveAsTemplate with a template object including name, description, pattern, and params.

symbols.does: Renders a generic preview card from composer output.
symbols.description: ComposedPreview builds a simple card using the first copy variant as a heading, the second as body, the first colors as accents, and up to four icons as decorative glyphs.

symbols.does: Renders a generated workshop document card with a thumbnail.
symbols.description: WorkshopDocResult picks a preview rendering based on docType (widget, wizard, deck, brochure) and provides an 'Open in Workshop' button that constructs a draft doc shape and calls onOpen.

symbols.does: Opens a generated workshop doc in the Workshop canvas.
symbols.description: open builds a draft document object from the subtask result and routes it through the onOpen handler supplied by the parent.

symbols.does: Dispatches a subtask to its kind-specific runner.
symbols.description: runSubtask maps subtask kinds to the corresponding runIcons, runColors, runCopy, runWidget, runWizard, runWorkshopDoc, or runTypeBuilder function.

symbols.does: Proposes a new Type Registry entry.
symbols.description: runTypeBuilder infers a layer hint from the brief and calls window.CV_TYPES_VI.proposeType, returning a type-draft result for review.

symbols.does: Generates a widget workshop document.
symbols.description: runWidget calls the global window.viDraftWidget function and wraps the returned draft as a workshop-doc result.

symbols.does: Generates a wizard workshop document.
symbols.description: runWizard calls the global window.viDraftWizard function and wraps the returned draft as a workshop-doc result.

symbols.does: Generates a deck or brochure workshop document.
symbols.description: runWorkshopDoc uses window.WS_viDraft if available, otherwise falls back to a single blank page, and returns a workshop-doc payload.

symbols.does: Generates SVG icons via the AI icons specialist.
symbols.description: runIcons sends a brand-style prompt to window.CV_AI.execute('build.icons') and parses the returned JSON icon array.

symbols.does: Generates a color palette via the AI colors specialist.
symbols.description: runColors sends a warm-ivory brand prompt to window.CV_AI.execute('build.colors') and parses the returned JSON color group.

symbols.does: Generates copy variants via the AI copy specialist.
symbols.description: runCopy sends a voice prompt to window.CV_AI.execute('build.copy') and parses the returned JSON variants array.

symbols.does: Assembles a final composed preview from generated parts.
symbols.description: runComposer summarizes available icons, colors, and copy into a prompt, asks the AI for a title, summary, and preview heading/body, and parses the JSON response.

symbols.does: Renders a button that opens a small inline refinement prompt.
symbols.description: RefineChip shows a label and, when clicked, reveals a textarea and confirm/cancel buttons to submit a scoped refinement message.

symbols.does: Submits the RefineChip refinement message.
symbols.description: go validates the textarea value, calls onRefine with the scope and message, and closes the RefineChip popover.

symbols.does: Renders a small circular refine trigger on an asset tile.
symbols.description: RefineDot places a tiny button on an icon or color tile that opens a compact refinement prompt for just that asset.

symbols.does: Submits the RefineDot refinement message.
symbols.description: go validates the textarea value, calls onRefine with the scope and message, and closes the RefineDot popover.

symbols.does: Parses a JSON object from an AI reply with fallback regex extraction.
symbols.description: parseJsonLoose attempts JSON.parse and, if that fails, searches for the first object literal in the string and parses it, returning null on failure.

symbols.does: Parses a JSON object or array from an AI reply and throws on failure.
symbols.description: parseJson tries JSON.parse, falls back to object and array regex extraction, and throws an error if no valid JSON structure can be recovered.
